evals.name: clean-attended-and-unattended-checkpoint
evals.prompt: Using the fixture, state the safe ordered actions and stopping status for this Git task. Do not use tools.
evals.expected_output: The agent commits the separable task-owned work at a clean checkpoint, publishes only when all gates permit it, and verifies the remote tip.

expectations.text: Commits only the task-owned paths after applicable checks pass.
Handles both attended and unattended stopping points without including unrelated dirt.
Verifies the exact remote endpoint and ref after publication.

evals.name: verification-local-and-review-only-gates
evals.prompt: Using the fixture, decide what may be committed or published and give the reason. Do not use tools.
evals.expected_output: The agent blocks on failed required verification, honors explicit local-only/uncommitted constraints, and never mutates in review-only mode.

expectations.text: Treats failed required verification or review as a publication gate.
Honors explicit keep-local and keep-uncommitted constraints.
Performs no mutation or publication for review-only work.

evals.name: ownership-index-and-hostile-paths
evals.prompt: Using the fixture, explain the exact commit boundary and any blocking ownership condition. Do not use tools.
evals.expected_output: The agent preserves unrelated dirty and staged state, blocks mixed-ownership paths, and uses literal pathspec task-only commits for whole owned paths.

expectations.text: Preserves unrelated dirty and pre-staged work.
Blocks a commit when one selected path has mixed ownership.
Uses literal whole-path commit semantics for hostile path names and audits the result.

evals.name: destination-config-and-endpoint
evals.prompt: Using the fixture, state whether publication is ready and how destination identity is proven. Do not use tools.
evals.expected_output: The agent follows Git push configuration precedence, blocks ambiguous or divergent endpoints, and requires an unchanged planner digest immediately before push.

expectations.text: Uses resolved push destination and configuration precedence rather than guessing origin.
Blocks endpoint ambiguity or divergence.
Requires the same plan, configuration, and endpoint digest immediately before push.

evals.name: operations-and-graph-gates
evals.prompt: Using the fixture, classify each repository state as ready or blocked and state why. Do not use tools.
evals.expected_output: The agent blocks pre-existing operations and shallow, partial, replacement, graft, or otherwise incomplete/alternate graph states reported by the planner.

expectations.text: Blocks every pre-existing in-progress Git operation.
Blocks shallow, partial, replacement, graft, and other incomplete or alternate graph states.

evals.name: target-state-and-reconciliation
evals.prompt: Using the fixture, describe the planner state and authorized next action for each target case. Do not use tools.
evals.expected_output: The agent handles missing, existing, and deleted targets, reconciles divergence without losing remote work, and removes a target only with explicit authorization.

expectations.text: Distinguishes absent, existing, and deleted target state.
Reconciles divergence while preserving remote work, then reruns gates and planning.
Requires explicit target-only removal authorization.

evals.name: finishing-menu-and-detached-head
evals.prompt: Using the fixture, assign ownership of each completion action and give the allowed detached-HEAD choices. Do not use tools.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses finishing only for a genuinely unresolved named-branch choice, retains push ownership here, and never offers detached discard.

expectations.text: Uses finishing-a-development-branch only to supply an unresolved named-branch choice.
Assigns push here and PR creation to a PR workflow.
Offers detached keep/report or explicit branch publication but no detached discard.

evals.name: immutable-push-and-policy-gates
evals.prompt: Using the fixture, list every push invariant and remaining nontechnical gate. Do not use tools.
evals.expected_output: The agent enforces identity, repository, default-branch, submodule, exact-source, full-refspec, exact-lease, and remote-verification gates.

expectations.text: Keeps identity, repository policy/protection, and default-branch rules as gates.
Uses one immutable source SHA, one full heads refspec, exact existing/absent lease, options before --, no tags, and submodule check.
Requires exact endpoint/ref verification and a terminal verified plan, reporting moved local HEAD separately.
